title: cerinthe glabra subsp. pirinica
intro: Cerinthe glabra subsp. pirinica, also known as Balkan hawk's beard, is a rare and endemic plant species native to Bulgaria, Greece, and Turkey. It is a biennial herb that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae), characterized by its greyish-green leaves and small, blue-violet flowers. Cerinthe glabra subsp. pirinica has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health, and it can be consumed through herbal teas, infusions, or dried extracts.
